source_jobs.title: Senior Sales Executive, Federal
source_jobs.description:
SimSpace serves as an AI Proving Ground where organizations can confidently train, test, and outmaneuver adversaries in any environment. Trusted by allied governments, militaries, enterprises, and research institutions worldwide, SimSpace enables adaptive, AI-ready defenses that stay ahead of evolving threats. Founded in 2015 by experts from U.S. Cyber Command and MIT Lincoln Laboratory, the platform unifies training, testing, and validation in a realistic, live-fire simulation—helping teams evaluate security investments, optimize performance, and compress cyber readiness cycles from months to days.



Why join SimSpace? We are an organization that is focused on building our culture and mindfully enhancing our atmosphere every day which is why we have collaborated on an integral value system. Our governing philosophy of being Human Centered is deeply embedded within our value system. We apply this philosophy to every one of our internal team members, external clients, and their customers.



How Do We Work? We believe that people are at the center of everything we do. SimSpace fosters a culture of continuous learning, curiosity, and professional growth. That belief shows up in action: in-house training, internal and external learning platforms, cyber conferences, industry events, and dedicated time for skill development. Our people are empowered to shape their careers - and it shows. Year over year, SimSpace consistently outperforms industry benchmarks in internal mobility, promotions, and total rewards growth.



Who Thrives Here? We are a team of innovators, protectors, and problem-solvers. We believe diversity of thought and experience fuels better solutions, and we’re committed to building teams that reflect the communities we serve. Whether you’re remote or office-based, you’ll collaborate with talented colleagues across departments and time zones, united by the mission to create a safer digital world.



We invite you to apply today!



What will you be doing as a Senior Sales Executive (Federal) at SimSpace?

As a Senior Sales Executive (Federal) at SimSpace, you will lead new business development across U.S. Federal civilian, defense, and intelligence agencies. Your focus will be identifying, qualifying, and closing opportunities where agencies will leverage the SimSpace platform to improve cyber readiness through individual & team training, evaluate and tune security tools and build, train & validate AI agents in complex, real-world environments.

This is a quota-carrying role responsible for building pipeline, navigating federal procurement processes, and closing complex, multi-stakeholder deals. You will work cross-functionally with Solutions Architects, Customer Success, Marketing, and Engineering to drive deal strategy and execution.

You will report directly to the Chief Revenue Officer.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - Build and execute a federal sales strategy across a defined set of agencies or accounts

 - Develop and close net-new business opportunities within U.S. Federal organizations

 - Navigate complex procurement pathways and multi-stakeholder buying processes

 - Establish trusted relationships with technical, program, and contracting stakeholders

 - Maintain accurate pipeline and forecasting discipline

 - Consistently meet or exceed quota targets

Who you are:

 - You have a track record of closing new business within U.S. Federal agencies, specifically in cybersecurity, defense, or AI-driven technologies

 - You understand federal procurement pathways (e.g., IDIQs, OTAs, SBIR, prime/sub relationships) and how to navigate them

 - You have existing relationships and have a successful track record with large and small Federal/Defense Contractors

 - You’ve built relationships with key stakeholders across technical teams, program leadership, and contracting offices

 - You can independently develop and execute account strategies across a defined territory or set of agencies

 - You are comfortable sel

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager of Product Marketing
source_jobs.description:
SimSpace serves as an AI Proving Ground where organizations can confidently train, test, and outmaneuver adversaries in any environment. Trusted by allied governments, militaries, enterprises, and research institutions worldwide, SimSpace enables adaptive, AI-ready defenses that stay ahead of evolving threats. Founded in 2015 by experts from U.S. Cyber Command and MIT Lincoln Laboratory, the platform unifies training, testing, and validation in a realistic, live-fire simulation—helping teams evaluate security investments, optimize performance, and compress cyber readiness cycles from months to days.



Why join SimSpace? We are an organization that is focused on building our culture and mindfully enhancing our atmosphere every day which is why we have collaborated on an integral value system. Our governing philosophy of being Human Centered is deeply embedded within our value system. We apply this philosophy to every one of our internal team members, external clients, and their customers.



How Do We Work? We believe that people are at the center of everything we do. SimSpace fosters a culture of continuous learning, curiosity, and professional growth. That belief shows up in action: in-house training, internal and external learning platforms, cyber conferences, industry events, and dedicated time for skill development. Our people are empowered to shape their careers - and it shows. Year over year, SimSpace consistently outperforms industry benchmarks in internal mobility, promotions, and total rewards growth.



Who Thrives Here? We are a team of innovators, protectors, and problem-solvers. We believe diversity of thought and experience fuels better solutions, and we’re committed to building teams that reflect the communities we serve. Whether you’re remote or office-based, you’ll collaborate with talented colleagues across departments and time zones, united by the mission to create a safer digital world.



We invite you to apply today!



About SimSpace
Innovators from US Cyber Command and MIT Lincoln Laboratory founded SimSpace in 2015 to develop next gen offensive and defensive capabilities for the most elite units of the US military and intelligence communities.

Allied governments, militaries, commercial enterprises, and research universities worldwide adopted the SimSpace cyber range to train human operators and to test security tools and weapons systems in a realistic replica of their production environments.

Today, organizations around the globe trust SimSpace as their AI Proving Grounds to converge training and testing in a unified cyber simulation platform to outperform and outsmart any adversary in any terrain.


About The Position
We are seeking a hands-on, individual contributor Senior Manager of Product Marketing with 5-7 years of product marketing experience in cybersecurity to partner with Product and Sales to ensure we adhere to our internal motto of: “sell what we build, build what we sell, and market what we sell and build.”



This position requires a strategic thinker with hands-on expertise in all aspects of product marketing. As an individual contributor, you’ll bring a genuine interest and natural tendency to collaborate closely across Marketing, Product, Sales as well as Customer Success. Your primary focus will be to distill our messaging and positioning into first-class externally-facing playbooks to enable Sales and product-centric content assets to fuel middle-of-funnel demand generation campaigns by explaining in technical detail how we uniquely deliver our differentiated value.



In your first few months, you’ll learn the product portfolio inside and out so you can speak credibly and convincingly to our technical audiences. You’ll also closely study our market to learn the nuances of our space and to gain an appreciation for our positioning, value-based messaging, and what truly differentiates us. You’ll then dig into our ideal customer profile and primary buyer personas, connect with key Sa

Our people are empowered to shape their careers - and it shows. Year over year, SimSpace consistently outperforms industry benchmarks in internal mobility, promotions, and total rewards growth.

We believe diversity of thought and experience fuels better solutions, and we’re committed to building teams that reflect the communities we serve. Whether you’re remote or office-based, you’ll collaborate with talented colleagues across departments and time zones, united by the mission to create a safer digital world.



We invite you to apply today!



SimSpace is seeking a Senior UX Designer II to help shape the future of our application suite. This person will reside in the Boston area, mainly work remotely and at times join the team for meetings in-person.



This role is ideal for a designer who thrives in complex domains, enjoys turning ambiguity into clarity, and can confidently partner with Product Management and Engineering to define and deliver exceptional user experiences.



Success in this role requires strong systems thinking, a customer-centered mindset, and the ability to balance rapid exploration with thoughtful execution. You'll work across challenging enterprise workflows, contribute to our evolving design system, and help drive usability and consistency throughout the application.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Partner closely with Product Managers and Engineers to discover, define, and deliver valuable customer solutions.

 - Translate complex workflows, systems, and technical concepts into intuitive and effective user experiences.

 - Use rapid, low-fidelity exploration techniques—including sketches, diagrams, workflows, and prototypes—to evaluate ideas, align stakeholders, and accelerate decision-making.

 - Design end-to-end experiences through information architecture, user flows, wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity designs.

 - Conduct user research, usability testing, and design validation activities to inform design decisions.

 - Independently lead UX initiatives and drive solutions for complex product challenges.

 - Apply established design patterns and contribute to the evolution of our design system to improve consistency, accessibility, and scalability.

 - Collaborate with fellow designers through critique, feedback, and shared problem-solving.

 - Present design work and rationale clearly to stakeholders, leadership, and cross-functional teams.

 - Leverage AI-powered tools and emerging technologies to accelerate research, ideation, design exploration, documentation, and execution.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS

SimSpace is investing in the craft, consistency, and quality of every interface our customers touch, as well as in the systems that make great UI possible at scale. Our Core UI team owns the foundation the rest of the product is built on: our design system, shared component libraries, frontend architecture, and the partnership between engineering and UX Design.



As a Staff Software Engineer on Core UI, you'll be the senior-most technical voice on the team—setting frontend architecture and standards, leading complex UI initiatives end to end, and contributing directly to the systems every product team depends on. You'll partner deeply with UX Designers, Product Managers, and engineers across the organization, and your technical leadership and mentorship will be key to helping the team raise its bar.



This is a hands-on technical leadership role, not a people-management one. You set direction through architecture, code, and influence—staying close enough to the work to make the hard calls, unblock difficult problems, and hold a high bar for craft, accessibility, and performance. We're looking for an experienced engineer who wants real ownership of a domain and treats the frontend platform as a product.



WHAT YOU'LL DO



SET TECHNICAL DIRECTION & ARCHITECTURE

 - Serve as the senior-most technical voice for SimSpace's core frontend, owning architecture decisions and the technical roadmap for shared UI systems.

 - Lead major frontend initiatives end to end—from requirements gathering and architecture design through delivery—setting clear goals, priorities, and technical direction.

 - Steward architectural decisions and manage technical and UX debt deliberately, balancing long-term health with delivery needs.

 - Define and enforce frontend technical standards for code health, performance, testing, and documentation.

 - Mentor Design Engineers and frontend engineers, modeling strong craft, collaboration, and pragmatism.


BUILD & EVOLVE CORE UI SYSTEMS

We invite you to apply today!



A Senior META Solution Engineer to help drive SimSpace’s growth across the Middle East, Turkey, and Africa region by serving as a trusted technical advisor to customers, prospects, partners, resellers, and system integrators. Must also be capable of traveling to and from the European region to manage European operations.

This role requires a technically credible, commercially minded professional who understands the cybersecurity priorities, buying culture, partner ecosystem, and client expectations across META and Europe. The ideal candidate can translate SimSpace’s advanced cyber range capabilities into clear mission value for government, defense, critical infrastructure, energy, financial services, telecommunications, aviation, and large enterprise customers.

You will partner closely with regional sales, product, customer success, professional services, and partner teams to lead technical discovery, deliver tailored demonstrations, shape evaluations, support RFI/RFP responses, and help customers understand how SimSpace can improve cyber readiness, validate security capabilities, train teams, test technologies, and emulate real-world threats.

The ideal candidate is fluent in business English and Arabic, with the ability to engage both executive and technical stakeholders across the region. Familiarity with Turkish, French, or additional regional languages is a plus. This individual should be comfortable operating across cultures, engaging senior government and enterprise stakeholders, and representing SimSpace as a regional thought leader through webinars, conferences, executive briefings, and partner-led events.

 
 


WHAT WILL YOU BE DOING AS A SENIOR META SOLUTION ENGINEER AT SIMSPACE?

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Identity & Access Management
source_jobs.description:

Trusted by allied governments, militaries, enterprises, and research institutions worldwide, SimSpace enables adaptive, AI-ready defenses that stay ahead of evolving threats. Our people are empowered to shape their careers - and it shows. Year over year, SimSpace consistently outperforms industry benchmarks in internal mobility, promotions, and total rewards growth.



Who Thrives Here? We are a team of innovators, protectors, and problem-solvers. We believe diversity of thought and experience fuels better solutions, and we’re committed to building teams that reflect the communities we serve. Whether you’re remote or office-based, you’ll collaborate with talented colleagues across departments and time zones, united by the mission to create a safer digital world.



We invite you to apply today!



We are looking for a Staff Software Engineer, Identity & Access Management, to serve as the technical authority for identity, authentication, and authorization across the SimSpace platform. The ideal candidate will possess deep expertise in designing and building secure, scalable software systems, a strong foundation in modern IAM concepts, and the ability to drive technical direction across teams in a complex, security-focused environment.



In this position, you'll own the architecture and technical strategy for the IAM stack, partnering with engineering teams across the organization to establish authn/authz standards and ensure consistent, secure access patterns throughout the SimSpace platform. The focus is on software engineering leadership — designing and building the services that underpin identity and access management at SimSpace, solving hard problems, and raising the engineering bar across the team. Specifically, this position will be responsible for:

 - Identity Provider architecture and service development built on Keycloak

 - Authorization policy design and enforcement using a Relationship-Based Access Control (ReBAC) model implemented in Topaz/OPA

 - Design and development of IAM-adjacent services including directory services, user management, and other platform integrations that augment the core identity stack

 - Cross-team authn/authz standards, patterns, and platform integrations




WHAT WILL YOU BE DOING AS A STAFF SOFTWARE ENGINEER, IAM AT SIMSPACE?

 - Define and own the technical architecture for authentication and authorization across the SimSpace platform, ensuring systems are secure, scalable, and maintainable.

 - Lead the design and development of Keycloak-based identity infrastructure, including federation, SSO, token management, and multi-tenant identity flows — multi-tenancy is a core architectural concern and experience designing systems with strong tenant isolation is highly valued.

 - Design and build the authorization layer for the SimSpace platfor

source_jobs.title: Principal Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
SimSpace serves as an AI Proving Ground where organizations can confidently train, test, and outmaneuver adversaries in any environment. Trusted by allied governments, militaries, enterprises, and research institutions worldwide, SimSpace enables adaptive, AI-ready defenses that stay ahead of evolving threats. Founded in 2015 by experts from U.S. Cyber Command and MIT Lincoln Laboratory, the platform unifies training, testing, and validation in a realistic, live-fire simulation—helping teams evaluate security investments, optimize performance, and compress cyber readiness cycles from months to days.



Why join SimSpace? We are an organization that is focused on building our culture and mindfully enhancing our atmosphere every day which is why we have collaborated on an integral value system. Our governing philosophy of being Human Centered is deeply embedded within our value system. We apply this philosophy to every one of our internal team members, external clients, and their customers.



How Do We Work? We believe that people are at the center of everything we do. SimSpace fosters a culture of continuous learning, curiosity, and professional growth. That belief shows up in action: in-house training, internal and external learning platforms, cyber conferences, industry events, and dedicated time for skill development. Our people are empowered to shape their careers - and it shows. Year over year, SimSpace consistently outperforms industry benchmarks in internal mobility, promotions, and total rewards growth.



Who Thrives Here? We are a team of innovators, protectors, and problem-solvers. We believe diversity of thought and experience fuels better solutions, and we’re committed to building teams that reflect the communities we serve. Whether you’re remote or office-based, you’ll collaborate with talented colleagues across departments and time zones, united by the mission to create a safer digital world.



We invite you to apply today!



Summary

As a Principal Product Manager at SimSpace, you will help define the future of cybersecurity and AI readiness. SimSpace provides advanced, realistic cyber ranges as an AI Proving Grounds for mission critical organizations worldwide. This role is strategic in leading the development of our platform to enable organizations to train defenders, evaluate security controls, rehearse missions, test operational resilience, and validate emerging AI and agentic systems under realistic conditions.



AI systems and autonomous agents are rapidly becoming part of how organizations defend, operate, automate, and make decisions. As adoption accelerates, enterprises and government organizations need trusted environments to understand how these systems perform, where they fail, how they behave under adversarial pressure, and whether they can be safely deployed in mission-critical workflows. SimSpace is uniquely positioned to provide the realistic cyber environments, instrumentation, emulation, and validation workflows required to support that transformation.



In this role, you will define and drive platform capabilities that support AI training, testing, evaluation, and validation within realistic cyber environments. The role requires a product leader who can operate at the intersection of AI systems, cybersecurity, enterprise platform architecture, simulation, automation, and customer outcomes. You will partner across engineering, design, cyber subject matter experts, go-to-market teams, and customers to ensure SimSpace products are technically sound, commercially relevant, strategically differentiated, and measurable in the value they deliver.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


PRODUCT STRATEGY & ROADMAP

 - Define and communicate the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for platform capabilities that support AI Proving Grounds, agent validation, cyber tool efficacy, security automation, and realistic cyber training and testing.

 - Identify customer, market, and technology trends at the intersection

source_jobs.title: Backend Senior Software Engineer (Control Plane & Orchestration)
source_jobs.description:
SimSpace serves as an AI Proving Ground where organizations can confidently train, test, and outmaneuver adversaries in any environment. Trusted by allied governments, militaries, enterprises, and research institutions worldwide, SimSpace enables adaptive, AI-ready defenses that stay ahead of evolving threats. Founded in 2015 by experts from U.S. Cyber Command and MIT Lincoln Laboratory, the platform unifies training, testing, and validation in a realistic, live-fire simulation—helping teams evaluate security investments, optimize performance, and compress cyber readiness cycles from months to days.



Why join SimSpace? We are an organization that is focused on building our culture and mindfully enhancing our atmosphere every day which is why we have collaborated on an integral value system. Our governing philosophy of being Human Centered is deeply embedded within our value system. We apply this philosophy to every one of our internal team members, external clients, and their customers.



How Do We Work? We believe that people are at the center of everything we do. SimSpace fosters a culture of continuous learning, curiosity, and professional growth. That belief shows up in action: in-house training, internal and external learning platforms, cyber conferences, industry events, and dedicated time for skill development. Our people are empowered to shape their careers - and it shows. Year over year, SimSpace consistently outperforms industry benchmarks in internal mobility, promotions, and total rewards growth.



Who Thrives Here? We are a team of innovators, protectors, and problem-solvers. We believe diversity of thought and experience fuels better solutions, and we’re committed to building teams that reflect the communities we serve. Whether you’re remote or office-based, you’ll collaborate with talented colleagues across departments and time zones, united by the mission to create a safer digital world.



We invite you to apply today!



Senior Software Engineer, Control Plane & Orchestration

We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer to build the orchestration engine at the heart of SimSpace's internet simulation platform. Every action in the platform, standing up a simulated service, registering a persona, pushing a topology update to a range, delivering a file to a scenario, provisioning a SaaS application, flows through the control plane this engineer owns.



Where the other engineering roles on the team build the simulation layers themselves, this role builds the system that ties them together: the API surface that external systems call, the event-driven backbone that coordinates actions across subsystems, and the deployment and operational model that keeps a complex, multi-tenant platform running reliably across many concurrent range deployments.



If you are energized by building systems that other engineers depend on and have strong instincts for distributed systems correctness and operational rigor, this role is for you.



The team is responsible for:

• The control plane API: the external-facing interface through which range orchestration, scenario management, and user emulation systems drive the internet simulation platform, creating services, registering personas, updating topology, and querying state

• The event-driven orchestration backbone that coordinates actions across the platform's simulation layers, network, services, identity, and storage, and maintains consistent state across a distributed, multi-range deployment

• Persistent storage infrastructure for the platform: service data, content corpora, certificate stores, topology state, and the volumes that simulated services depend on

• The deployment and operational model for the internet simulation platform itself as a long-lived Kubernetes-native product, including upgrade paths, multi-range scaling, and the configuration model for new range integrations

• In-range proxy agent infrastructure in collaboration with the network simulation tech

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer (Attack & User Emulation Team)
source_jobs.description:
SimSpace serves as an AI Proving Ground where organizations can confidently train, test, and outmaneuver adversaries in any environment. Trusted by allied governments, militaries, enterprises, and research institutions worldwide, SimSpace enables adaptive, AI-ready defenses that stay ahead of evolving threats. Whether you’re remote or office-based, you’ll collaborate with talented colleagues across departments and time zones, united by the mission to create a safer digital world.



We invite you to apply today!



We are looking for: A Staff Software Engineer for our Attack and User Emulation (AUE) Team to provide expertise in microservices, event-driven messaging systems, Spring Boot, and containerization. In this role, you will drive delivery of various containerized microservices that provide the core activity emulation services of our cyber ranges. These services allow our users to simulate real-life cyber attacks and user personas to add high fidelity realism to our products.



You'll be responsible for setting technical direction across the team, guiding engineering practices, system architecture, and cultivating a culture of continuous improvement. Your mentorship will be key in helping senior and mid-level team members grow, while you contribute directly to code, design, and architecture. You will be expected to operate with a high degree of autonomy, define solutions to ambiguous problems, and influence technical decisions beyond your immediate team.



What will you be doing as our Staff Software Engineer on the AUE team:

 - Design, develop, and maintain the core services for offensive cybersecurity and user emulation in our range simulation environment, focusing on scalability, performance, and fault tolerance.

 - Own and drive end-to-end technical projects, from requirements gathering and architecture design to deployment. This includes defining scope, identifying risks, and aligning stakeholders across teams, while setting clear goals and priorities.

 - Mentor and support engineers on the team; promote a collaborative culture and guide the team in coding best practices, testing, and architectural design. Actively develop the technical skills of senior engineers and help raise the overall engineering bar on the team.

 - Define and evangelize technical architecture, system design, and best practices through documentation, design reviews, and team forums to ensure knowledge sharing and alignment.

 - Collaborate with Product Managers and UX Designers to create user-centered solutions

source_jobs.description:

Whether you’re remote or office-based, you’ll collaborate with talented colleagues across departments and time zones, united by the mission to create a safer digital world.



We invite you to apply today!



Technical Alliances Manager – Security Ecosystem & Product Partnerships

Location: Remote (US-based preferred)
Reports to: Global Director, Business Development / Partnerships
Travel: ~20–30% (partner meetings, industry events, customer sessions)

Role Overview

SimSpace is seeking a Technical Alliances Manager to build and scale our third-party security technology ecosystem within the SimSpace Cyber Range. This role is critical to enabling realistic, high-fidelity customer use-cases by sourcing, licensing, and supporting leading security applications across endpoint, network, identity, cloud, and data security.

The ideal candidate sits at the intersection of security technology, product management, and strategic partnerships. You will work directly with security vendors to educate them on how their products are used inside SimSpace for customer simulations, product testing, agentic SOC workflows, and go-to-market enablement—while planting the foundation for deeper partnerships, co-development, and future sell-to or sell-with relationships.

This role is highly cross-functional, partnering closely with SimSpace Product Management, Engineering, Sales Engineering, and Go-to-Market teams.

Key Responsibilities

Security Technology Sourcing & Ecosystem Development

 - Maintain a curated security technology roadmap aligned to SimSpace product strategy and customer demand.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer, Network Simulations
source_jobs.description:
SimSpace serves as an AI Proving Ground where organizations can confidently train, test, and outmaneuver adversaries in any environment. Trusted by allied governments, militaries, enterprises, and research institutions worldwide, SimSpace enables adaptive, AI-ready defenses that stay ahead of evolving threats. Founded in 2015 by experts from U.S. Cyber Command and MIT Lincoln Laboratory, the platform unifies training, testing, and validation in a realistic, live-fire simulation—helping teams evaluate security investments, optimize performance, and compress cyber readiness cycles from months to days.



Why join SimSpace? We are an organization that is focused on building our culture and mindfully enhancing our atmosphere every day which is why we have collaborated on an integral value system. Our governing philosophy of being Human Centered is deeply embedded within our value system. We apply this philosophy to every one of our internal team members, external clients, and their customers.



How Do We Work? We believe that people are at the center of everything we do. SimSpace fosters a culture of continuous learning, curiosity, and professional growth. That belief shows up in action: in-house training, internal and external learning platforms, cyber conferences, industry events, and dedicated time for skill development. Our people are empowered to shape their careers - and it shows. Year over year, SimSpace consistently outperforms industry benchmarks in internal mobility, promotions, and total rewards growth.



Who Thrives Here? We are a team of innovators, protectors, and problem-solvers. We believe diversity of thought and experience fuels better solutions, and we’re committed to building teams that reflect the communities we serve. Whether you’re remote or office-based, you’ll collaborate with talented colleagues across departments and time zones, united by the mission to create a safer digital world.



We invite you to apply today!



We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer to serve as the technical lead for SimSpace's internet simulation platform — the system that makes cyber range environments behave like the real internet at the network layer. This is foundational, novel work: you will be designing and building a kernel-level simulation layer that reproduces the observable behavior of worldwide internet routing — correct ASN topology, realistic inter-AS latency, accurate traceroute and MTR output — without instantiating a single virtual router.



The approach is grounded in a patent pending eBPF-based architecture developed at SimSpace, and this role will take that proof of concept into a production-grade system. As the technical lead on a newly formed team, you will set architectural direction, mentor peer engineers, and partner closely with a Principal Engineer and your Engineering Manager to build something that does not yet exist at this fidelity in the industry.



The team is responsible for:

 - A persistent, control-plane internet simulation service that serves multiple concurrent cyber ranges without per-range instantiation

 - An eBPF-based network simulation layer providing geographically accurate ASN routing, inter-AS latency enforcement, and traceroute and MTR fidelity entirely in kernel space

 - Lightweight in-range proxy agents that receive topology configuration from the control plane and handle local packet interception without requiring a full simulation stack in every range

 - A topology database encoding real-world ASN-to-CIDR mappings, inter-AS latency models, and geographic placement data that drives the simulation

 - Dynamic reconfiguration of the simulated internet topology at runtime without disrupting in-flight traffic

 

What will you be doing as a Senior Software Engineer, Network Simulation at SimSpace?

 - Own the end-to-end architecture of the eBPF-based routing simulation layer, including XDP and TC hook point design, BPF map structure, and synthetic ICMP TTL-exceeded gener

SENIOR NETWORK ENGINEER



SUMMARY OF THE ROLE

The Senior Network Engineer position is a senior technology practitioner responsible for the collaborative design, implementation, monitoring, maintenance, and security of enterprise network infrastructure across SimSpace corporate offices and data centers across the United States and Europe.

This individual will serve as a senior technical resource and subject matter expert for both core network and network security infrastructure, helping lead operational support, architecture, modernization, and incident response efforts. The role combines traditional network engineering responsibilities with expanded ownership of network security operations, secure access technologies, firewall management, web application protection, and vulnerability management.

The successful candidate will possess deep technical expertise in enterprise networking and security technologies, strong analytical and troubleshooting skills, and the ability to lead initiatives and mentor junior engineers. Effective collaboration with infrastructure, cloud, security, and business stakeholders is critical to success in this role.

 


WHAT YOU WILL DO (RESPONSIBILITIES)

 - Act as the subject matter expert for SimSpace network and network security infrastructure

 - Design, implement, support, and secure enterprise network infrastructure and software-defined networking solutions

 - Configure and maintain complex VMware environments leveraging NSX-T, DVS, and software-defined networking technologies

 - Contribute to the design and implementation of resilient, secure, and scalable network architectures

 - Configure and troubleshoot virtual networking, VLANs, VRFs, traffic forwarding, and hybrid cloud connectivity

 - Lead network modernization initiatives, including technology refreshes, architecture improvements and capacity planning

 - Troubleshoot all levels of network connectivity issues including, but not limited to, network outages, switch failures, network performa

We invite you to apply today!



We are looking for a Staff Software Engineer, Identity & Access Management, to serve as the technical authority for identity, authentication, and authorization across the SimSpace platform. The ideal candidate will possess deep expertise in designing and building secure, scalable software systems, a strong foundation in modern IAM concepts, and the ability to drive technical direction across teams in a complex, security-focused environment.



In this position, you'll own the architecture and technical strategy for the IAM stack, partnering with engineering teams across the organization to establish authn/authz standards and ensure consistent, secure access patterns throughout the SimSpace platform. The focus is on software engineering leadership — designing and building the services that underpin identity and access management at SimSpace, solving hard problems, and raising the engineering bar across the team. Specifically, this position will be responsible for:

 - Identity Provider architecture and service development built on Keycloak

 - Authorization policy design and enforcement using a Relationship-Based Access Control (ReBAC) model implemented in Topaz/OPA

 - Design and development of IAM-adjacent services including directory services, user management, and other platform integrations that augment the core identity stack

 - Cross-team authn/authz standards, patterns, and platform integrations




WHAT WILL YOU BE DOING AS A STAFF SOFTWARE ENGINEER, IAM AT SIMSPACE?

 - Define and own the technical architecture for authentication and authorization across the SimSpace platform, ensuring systems are secure, scalable, and maintainable.

 - Lead the design and development of Keycloak-based identity infrastructure, including federation, SSO, token management, and multi-tenant identity flows — multi-tenancy is a core architectural concern and experience designing systems with strong tenant isolation is highly valued.

 - Design and build the authorization layer for the SimSpace platfor

source_jobs.description:

Who Thrives Here? We are a team of innovators, protectors, and problem-solvers. We believe diversity of thought and experience fuels better solutions, and we’re committed to building teams that reflect the communities we serve. Whether you’re remote or office-based, you’ll collaborate with talented colleagues across departments and time zones, united by the mission to create a safer digital world.



We invite you to apply today!



We are looking for a Software Engineer to build and operate the simulated internet services layer of SimSpace's internet simulation platform. The network simulation layer handles routing and latency; this role handles the internet itself — deploying and operating a persistent catalog of simulated and real internet services (think: fake GitHub, fake social media, fake webmail, AWS, GCP, Azure, and dozens of real SaaS apps) that red and blue teams interact with.



This is a creative and technically broad role that combines service deployment automation, content generation, and DNS and email infrastructure to produce an internet environment that is indistinguishable from the real thing at the application layer.



The team is responsible for:

 - A persistent catalog of simulated internet services including open-source self-hosted platforms (social media, file sharing, webmail, collaboration tools, and others) and custom services that approximate major internet properties

 - Incorporate hooks to bring real internet SaaS applications seamlessly into the simulated internet

 - Simulate and/or bridge to popular cloud services in AWS, GCP, Azure, and others

 - Content seeding and web scraping pipelines that populate simulated services with realistic data — repositories, social posts, email threads, user activity — making them plausible to human operators and automated tooling alike

 - External DNS infrastructure: the internet simulation platform is the authoritative resolver for all simulated public domains, with correctly configured mail, sender authentication, and domain registration records

 - The email delivery boundary between the internet simulation platform and in-range mail infrastructure, enabling realistic and scenario-driven email including adversarial content for red team exercises

 - Service lifecycle automation: deploying, configuring, and managing simulated services in response to range and scenario requirements



What will you be doing as a Software Engineer, Internet Serv

About the Role We are looking for a Staff Site Reliability Engineer to define the technical vision, lead the architecture, and secure the infrastructure that powers the SimSpace cyber range platform. The ideal candidate is a deeply experienced SRE and exceptional software engineer who thinks strategically about distributed systems, reliability, and operability at a global scale. At the Staff level, you will act as a force multiplier—architecting resilient systems, driving engineering standards, and solving our most complex infrastructure challenges rather than relying on manual processes or localized fixes.

In this position, you'll provide overarching technical leadership across our SRE practice, bridging traditional site reliability, DevOps, and DevSecOps. You'll architect the systems and strategies that allow SimSpace to deliver software seamlessly across our own data centers, to customers who bring their own hardware, and as pre-packaged appliances with bundled hardware and software. As our on-premises product matures and scales, you will design the long-term automation frameworks that make these varied deployments robust, secure, and repeatable.

What will you be doing as a Staff SRE at SimSpace?

 - Technical Strategy & Architecture: Design and architect the overarching infrastructure strategy that enables consistent, repeatable, and secure deployments across SimSpace-hosted data centers, customer-provided hardware, and highly restricted air-gapped environments.

 - Platform Evolution & Configuration Management: Lead the evolution of our CI/CD and Kubernetes platforms. Drive advanced application packaging, templating, and configuration management strategies using Jsonnet and Grafana Tanka (alongside Kustomize). Move beyond maintaining pipelines to architecting multi-cluster, multi-environment deployment frameworks that drastically improve developer velocity.

We are looking for a Senior AI-Centric Threat Hunter

We are looking for a highly motivated Cybersecurity Professional to support mentorship and assessment of global security teams in basic to advanced cybersecurity skill areas. The ideal candidate has a strong technical background, is enthusiastic, is good at problem solving, has a strong desire to learn, prefers to be hands-on technically, and constantly strives for excellence.



The Senior AI-Centric Threat Hunter will be part of a team developing methodologies of incorporating AI agents into cybersecurity workflows, designing experiments to help develop/test AI agents and models, delivering instructional content to customers, and facilitating a variety of Blue Team exercises for SimSpace and our customers. When facilitating exercises, you will be responsible for teaching security concepts, demonstrating practical/hands-on use of open source and commercial security tools; referencing both well established and cutting edge techniques that Cybersecurity Incident Response Teams might use to effectively defend their networks from advanced cyber threats.


What will you be doing as a Senior AI-Centric Threat Hunter at SimSpace?

 - Lead an internal initiative to research and integrate candidate agentic capabilities into test environments

 - Serve as a consultant to customers looking to integrate agentic capabilities into their cybersecurity enterprise in how to identify, integrate and test capabilities prior to deployment

 - Keep up with industry evolution around AI and cybersecurity operations reviewing publications and standards

 - Bring world class mentorship of advanced threat hunting tactics and strategy for our global clients and international organizations.

 - Utilize years of Blue Teaming experience to establish parsing rules, dashboards, and tuning of numerous enterprise security tools in our cyber range platform to exceed client goals and objectives.

Senior Software Engineer, Control Plane & Orchestration

We are looking for a Senior Software Engineer to build the orchestration engine at the heart of SimSpace's internet simulation platform. Every action in the platform, standing up a simulated service, registering a persona, pushing a topology update to a range, delivering a file to a scenario, provisioning a SaaS application, flows through the control plane this engineer owns. Where the other engineering roles on the team build the simulation layers themselves, this role builds the system that ties them together: the API surface that external systems call, the event-driven backbone that coordinates actions across subsystems, and the deployment and operational model that keeps a complex, multi-tenant platform running reliably across many concurrent range deployments. If you are energized by building systems that other engineers depend on and have strong instincts for distributed systems correctness and operational rigor, this role is for you.



The team is responsible for:

 - The control plane API: the external-facing interface through which range orchestration, scenario management, and user emulation systems drive the internet simulation platform, creating services, registering personas, updating topology, and querying state

 - The event-driven orchestration backbone that coordinates actions across the platform's simulation layers, network, services, identity, and storage, and maintains consistent state across a distributed, multi-range deployment

 - Persistent storage infrastructure for the platform: service data, content corpora, certificate stores, topology state, and the volumes that simulated services depend on

 - The deployment and operational model for the internet simulation platform itself as a long-lived Kubernetes-native product, including upgrade paths, multi-range scaling, and the configuration model for new range integrations

 - In-range proxy agent infrastructure in collaboration with the network simulation tech

source_jobs.title: Manager, Hiring & Internal Mobility
source_jobs.description:
SimSpace serves as an AI Proving Ground where organizations can confidently train, test, and outmaneuver adversaries in any environment. Trusted by allied governments, militaries, enterprises, and research institutions worldwide, SimSpace enables adaptive, AI-ready defenses that stay ahead of evolving threats. Founded in 2015 by experts from U.S. Cyber Command and MIT Lincoln Laboratory, the platform unifies training, testing, and validation in a realistic, live-fire simulation—helping teams evaluate security investments, optimize performance, and compress cyber readiness cycles from months to days.



Why join SimSpace? We are an organization that is focused on building our culture and mindfully enhancing our atmosphere every day which is why we have collaborated on an integral value system. Our governing philosophy of being Human Centered is deeply embedded within our value system. We apply this philosophy to every one of our internal team members, external clients, and their customers.



How Do We Work? We believe that people are at the center of everything we do. SimSpace fosters a culture of continuous learning, curiosity, and professional growth. That belief shows up in action: in-house training, internal and external learning platforms, cyber conferences, industry events, and dedicated time for skill development. Our people are empowered to shape their careers - and it shows. Year over year, SimSpace consistently outperforms industry benchmarks in internal mobility, promotions, and total rewards growth.



Who Thrives Here? We are a team of innovators, protectors, and problem-solvers. We believe diversity of thought and experience fuels better solutions, and we’re committed to building teams that reflect the communities we serve. Whether you’re remote or office-based, you’ll collaborate with talented colleagues across departments and time zones, united by the mission to create a safer digital world.



We invite you to apply today!



👋 Hi, I'm Tabitha https://www.linkedin.com/in/tabitha-reilley-32463599/ - Head of Hiring at SimSpace. I lead our Talent function, which spans recruiting, talent operations, employee experience, internal mobility, learning and development, employee voice, culture initiatives, and the systems that power all of it.



At SimSpace, we believe hiring is one of the most important decisions we make. Every person we bring into the company shapes our culture, influences our ability to innovate, and impacts the experience of the people around them. That's why we've invested heavily in building a thoughtful, data-informed, and deeply human hiring function.



As our company continues to grow, I'm looking for a Manager, Hiring & Internal Mobility to partner with me on the day-to-day leadership of our global recruiting efforts while helping shape what's next.



This role is equal parts people leader, talent strategist, operator, and recruiter. You'll help lead a distributed team across the United States and Costa Rica, own some of our most important searches, and play a significant role in how we attract, assess, hire, develop, and retain talent across the organization.



If you're energized by helping people find meaningful careers, fascinated by what predicts long-term success, and excited about building modern hiring practices that challenge conventional thinking, I'd love to meet you.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This is a player-coach leadership role.

You'll directly manage a small but mighty team of Hiring & Internal Mobility Partners while carrying a strategic requisition load of your own. You'll spend time coaching recruiters, partnering with leaders, optimizing systems, improving hiring practices, and helping employees discover new opportunities within SimSpace.



We're not looking for someone who simply keeps the recruiting engine running. We're looking for someone who wants to improve it.



The right person will be comfortable moving between hiring strategy discussions with executives, sourcing

That belief shows up in action: in-house training, internal and external learning platforms, cyber conferences, industry events, and dedicated time for skill development. Our people are empowered to shape their careers - and it shows. Year over year, SimSpace consistently outperforms industry benchmarks in internal mobility, promotions, and total rewards growth.



Who Thrives Here? We are a team of innovators, protectors, and problem-solvers. We believe diversity of thought and experience fuels better solutions, and we’re committed to building teams that reflect the communities we serve. Whether you’re remote or office-based, you’ll collaborate with talented colleagues across departments and time zones, united by the mission to create a safer digital world.



We invite you to apply today!



This is an opportunity for currently contracted US based military professionals to apply for a remote 4-6 month temporary internship through the SkillBridge program.

What will you be doing as a Cyber Range Engineer at SimSpace?

 - Design and architect complex computer networks for testing and training

 - Ensure the networks are running properly with hosts, servers and services configured to dynamic customer or industry goals

 - Ensure the security tools are properly setup and configured

 - Ensure the traffic generators (SimSpace User Emulation) are properly modeling user behaviors

 - Ensure the attack generators (SimSpace Automated Attack Platform) are setup to execute the available attack scenarios

 - Troubleshoot cyber range components from networking, system administration, and SimSpace technologies utilizing a variety of technical methods

 - Participate in discussions with customers to help identify cyber range requirements based on their specific objectives and use cases

 - Work in an open communication collaboration environment that reaches across functional teams

 - Perform required tasks solely, in conjunction with other team members or vendors, as well as being able to take lead and accountability for specific tasks and projects

 - Perform required tasks quickly and efficiently during highly visible customer events

 - Utilize time management skills to deliver on time

What are the qualifications to apply? To be successful as a Cyber Range Engineer, you need to have:

 - 5+ years’ experience in a technical field

 - 2+ years of experience with VMware vCenter

 - 5+ years of experience running and maintaining complex Enterprise IT systems

 - 5+ years of experience deploying, operating, and configuring Windows and Linux

 - 3+ years of experience with a scripting language such as Python or PowerShell

 - An understanding of computer networking principles (OSI layers, Firewalls, VLANs, VPNs)

 - A strong understanding of cyber security tools (SIEM, Proxy, AV, EDR, DLP, H

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager (Core UI)
source_jobs.description:
SimSpace serves as an AI Proving Ground where organizations can confidently train, test, and outmaneuver adversaries in any environment. Trusted by allied governments, militaries, enterprises, and research institutions worldwide, SimSpace enables adaptive, AI-ready defenses that stay ahead of evolving threats. Founded in 2015 by experts from U.S. Cyber Command and MIT Lincoln Laboratory, the platform unifies training, testing, and validation in a realistic, live-fire simulation—helping teams evaluate security investments, optimize performance, and compress cyber readiness cycles from months to days.



Why join SimSpace? We are an organization that is focused on building our culture and mindfully enhancing our atmosphere every day which is why we have collaborated on an integral value system. Our governing philosophy of being Human Centered is deeply embedded within our value system. We apply this philosophy to every one of our internal team members, external clients, and their customers.



How Do We Work? We believe that people are at the center of everything we do. SimSpace fosters a culture of continuous learning, curiosity, and professional growth. That belief shows up in action: in-house training, internal and external learning platforms, cyber conferences, industry events, and dedicated time for skill development. Our people are empowered to shape their careers - and it shows. Year over year, SimSpace consistently outperforms industry benchmarks in internal mobility, promotions, and total rewards growth.



Who Thrives Here? We are a team of innovators, protectors, and problem-solvers. We believe diversity of thought and experience fuels better solutions, and we’re committed to building teams that reflect the communities we serve. Whether you’re remote or office-based, you’ll collaborate with talented colleagues across departments and time zones, united by the mission to create a safer digital world.



We invite you to apply today!



SimSpace is investing in the craft, consistency, and quality of every interface our customers touch, as well as in the systems that make great UI possible at scale. We're growing a dedicated Core UI engineering team that owns the foundation the rest of the product is built on: our design system, shared component libraries, frontend architecture, and the partnership between engineering and UX Design.



As the Engineering Manager for Core UI, you'll lead and grow this team—Design Engineers and Core Frontend Engineers—and be accountable for the health of our core frontend: the repositories, architecture, and standards everything else depends on, the relationship with UX Design, and the careers of the engineers who build it.



This is a people-leadership role with deep technical grounding. You lead through your team while staying close enough to the technical decisions to guide architecture, unblock hard problems, and hold a high bar for craft, accessibility, and performance. We're looking for a hungry, experienced engineering leader who wants real ownership—someone who treats the frontend platform as a product, sets a high standard, and drives frontend engineering forward in an owned, accountable way.



WHAT YOU'LL DO


BUILD AND LEAD THE CORE UI TEAM

 - Lead, grow, and develop a team of Design Engineers and Core Frontend Engineers; own hiring, onboarding, 1:1s, performance, and career growth.

 - Set the team's direction, goals, and priorities, translating platform strategy into a clear roadmap and execution plan.

 - Foster a culture that values collaboration, inclusion, craft, and creativity.

 - Mentor Design Engineers and frontend engineers, modeling strong craft, collaboration, and pragmatism.

 - Lead UI-focused initiatives end to end, balancing ideal UX outcomes with delivery realities.


OWN THE CORE FRONTEND PLATFORM

 - Own SimSpace's core frontend repositories, design system (e.g., Monorail), component libraries, and overall frontend architecture—treating the platform as a pr

notes: High volume of engineering and technical roles, with a focus on AI and cybersecurity. No explicit mention of sponsorship or clearance requirements.
